title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Dr. Haddock advises Miss Marple that living alone is risky due to her age and a touch of bronchitis. He suggests she replace her current caretaker, Miss Knight, with an old servant, noting that her nephew, Raymond, would pay a reasonable wage. After Dr. Haddock leaves to treat patients with common diseases—specifically measles, whooping cough, and suspected scarlet fever—Miss Marple becomes suspicious of his mention of these illnesses.

Miss Marple calls Mrs. Bantry to clarify a detail regarding Heather Badcock. Mrs. Bantry recalls Heather telling Marina Greg a story about having chickenpox, but Miss Marple questions if the illness was actually whooping cough. Mrs. Bantry dismisses the idea, suggesting it might have been hives instead. Following the call, Miss Marple considers whether her former parlour maid, Loyal Florence, could be persuaded to return to St. Mary Mead to care for her, though she doubts Florence would leave her cottage.

Shelley enters to collect dishes and attempts to comfort Miss Marple regarding the influence of Miss Knight. Shelley then proposes that she and her husband, Jim, move into the empty rooms near the kitchen—formerly the gardener's quarters—to care for Miss Marple. Shelley promises that Jim can assist with plumbing and carpentry, while she will learn housekeeping. Although Miss Marple doubts Shelley's domestic abilities, she is drawn to Shelley's eager and lively nature. Miss Marple agrees to consider the proposal, stating she will arrange for Miss Knight to move to a hotel in Lyndhurst to care for a Miss Conway, and requests to speak with Jim to discuss the details.
